LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, FEB-26-2019 HEARING HELD ON DEFENDANT CORE VENTURE CAPITAL LLC, KATHLEEN UTECHT'S MOTION TO STRIKE CERTAIN REQUESTS FOR RELIEF FROM PLAINTIFFS AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS CORE VENTURE CAPITAL LLC AND KATHLEEN UTECHT'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFF KERI FINDLEY'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED AS TO THE REQUEST FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES AND SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE REQUEST FOR INJUNCTIVE RELIEF. MS. FINDLEY HAS SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGED THAT DEFENDANTS ENGAGED IN MALICIOUS CONDUCT WITH A WILLFUL AND CONSCIOUS DISREGARD FOR THE RIGHTS OF OTHERS BY ALLEGING THAT DEFENDANTS CAUSED HER TERMINATION IN ORDER TO CONTINUE THEIR ILLICIT SCHEME. MS. FRIDLEY HAS NOT ALLEGED ONGOING CONDUCT BY DEFENDANTS OR ANY THREAT OF CONTINUING MISCONDUCT. (SEE MADRID V. PEROT SYSTEMS CORP. (2005) 130 CAL.APP.4TH 440, 465 ["INJUNCTIVE RELIEF HAS NO APPLICATION TO WRONGS WHICH HAVE BEEN COMPLETED, ABSENT A SHOWING THAT PAST VIOLATIONS WILL PROBABLY RECUR."].) PLAINTIFF IS GRANTED 20 DAYS TO AMEND TO ALLEGE SUCH ONGOING MISCONDUCT IF SHE CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH. (AS FULLY REPORTED IN THE REPORTER'S TRANSCRIPT) COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. (302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Certain Requests For Relief From Plaintiffs Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Feb-26-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Noah Hagey, Esq. 415-599-0211 of BraunHagey & Borden for Plaintiff.
Matthew Kahn, Esq. and Soolean Choy, Esq. 415-393-8200 of Gibson Dunn for Defendant CORE VENTURE CAPITAL LLC, KATHLEEN UTECHT.

RULING - The matter is Discussed. Party reports the matter is uncontested by both sides. (as fully reported in the reporter's notest) The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CORE VENTURE CAPITAL LLC, KATHLEEN UTECHT'S Motion To Strike Certain Requests For Relief From Plaintiffs Amended Complaint.

Defendants Core Venture Capital LLC and Kathleen Utecht's motion to strike portions of plaintiff Keri Findley's first amended complaint is denied as to the request for punitive damages and sustained with leave to amend as to the request for injunctive relief. Ms. Findley has sufficiently alleged that defendants engaged in malicious conduct with a willful and conscious disregard for the rights of others by alleging that defendants caused her termination in order to continue their illicit scheme. Ms. Fridley has not alleged ongoing conduct by Defendants or any threat of continuing misconduct. (See Madrid v. Perot Systems Corp. (2005) 130 Cal.App.4th 440, 465 ["Injunctive relief has no application to wrongs which have been completed, absent a showing that past violations will probably recur."].) Plaintiff is granted 20 days to amend to allege such ongoing misconduct if she can do so in good faith. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Counsel for Plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.
